summary: Iteration 10 (mentor profile management) tested end-to-end. 16/16 backend pytest cases pass and all frontend flows on /dashboard/profile and /app work as specified. PATCH /api/auth/profile correctly handles partial updates, image validation, empty-string clearing, email immutability, and Pydantic min_length for username/contact. /api/mobile/activate-license returns the new mentor_profile_image field (null when unset, data-URL when set). The Profile page UI loads, email is true read-only with lock icon, upload→preview live updates, Save persists, Remove+Save clears back to default ROBOT_IMG. On /app, the 230px EA avatar correctly switches between mobile-ea-avatar (when mentor has image) and mobile-ea-avatar-default (with scale 1.6 and objectPosition 50% 32% confirmed). No regressions on iteration 9 endpoints (activate-license, dashboard summary, admin stats).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py ProfileUpdateIn._validate_image is defined but never wired as a Pydantic @field_validator — it is currently dead code. The actual validation lives in update_profile() so behaviour is correct, but the unused classmethod is misleading. Either decorate it as @field_validator('profile_image') and remove the duplicate check in the route, or delete it.

Pydantic v2 BaseModel defaults to extra='ignore', which is why test_patch_email_field_ignored passes. If a future migration switches to extra='forbid' globally, this would start returning 422. Consider an explicit ConfigDict(extra='ignore') on ProfileUpdateIn to lock the contract.

Profile.jsx fileToCompressedDataUrl draws a black background fill before drawImage to handle transparent PNGs — fine for JPEG output, but worth noting the resulting EA avatar may have a black halo for circular logos with transparency. Cosmetic only.
server.py is now 1594 lines — still over the 700-line guideline. Splitting into routers/services would help maintainability.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (profile CRUD + upload + remove + /app avatar swap + default scale/position all verified)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@ea-central.com / Admin@123. Mentor: TEST_it6_ui_1778753851@test.com / Passw0rd! (approved, EA 'AlphaWave Pro', licence EAC-2F9D-E69F-6F75-CEB3). Mentor's username was changed to 'alpha_mentor' during testing; profile_image cleared in tearDown.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Mentor's profile_image was set then cleared back to null at end of tests. Username left as 'alpha_mentor' (matches iter9 retest state).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite test_iteration10.py covers all PATCH /auth/profile branches plus /mobile/activate-license mentor_profile_image plumbing. Use it as a regression baseline. The /app flow requires sending email → licence-key, fields use generic placeholders so prefer page.locator('input').first per step. After PATCH from a logged-in dashboard session via fetch(credentials:'include'), /app must be reloaded (not just re-navigated) to pick up the new mentor_profile_image because activate-license is a one-shot POST.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Implementation matches spec. Note one piece of dead code: ProfileUpdateIn._validate_image is an unwired classmethod (not a Pydantic v2 @field_validator), so it doesn't run — the equivalent check exists inline in update_profile() which is why behaviour is still correct.
